## [00:00 - 03:01]

Hey, I've got a really cool case study for you. This is with Shelly Brander and it's just an absolutely improbable case study for product launch formula and for online marketing. And you know what, Shelly's circumstances are probably different from yours, but I want to challenge you to watch what she did and think about how it can apply to your business. Because it's absolutely amazing how these strategies and tactics can apply to so many businesses. So this is going to, it's a real treat. You're going to love this one. Check it out. Knitting has been a passion of mine since I first learned to knit when I was 16 and then along the way I just got this crazy idea to open a yarn store. I've been in branding for 30 years. I have an ad agency with my husband and we started that 30 years ago. All my friends and family thought I was completely crazy to go from this really successful branding business and go out on this crazy limb and start a yarn store. I had no retail background whatsoever. I had no idea what I was doing. All I had in my back pocket was branding skills and a dream and I loved to knit. So the initial plan for the yarn store, I put together a business plan and it took me about two years to get into this shopping center that I really wanted to be in. The guy that owned the shopping center was really interested but I couldn't get him to give me a lease deal. And so I ordered this cashmere from New York and I made him a scarf and I dropped the scarf off on Christmas Eve with a proposal and New Year's Day I got the lease deal. It just kept haunting me like someday, like wow I could do this. I think I could open a yarn store. And I thought I can open a different kind of yarn store. I want to open a modern yarn store where you can walk in, find a great project really fast and then get back out the door and get started on it. We've totally created a community with Loops. It's really, it's like a Starbucks but even better. I mean you walk in, they say it's like cheers everyone knows your name when you walk in the door. And it's like a buzz that happens in the store that's just addictive and people just want to be here all the time. And that went along for about four years but we really were starting to struggle just because the retail business as a whole was struggling and it's also a really seasonal business. Yarn is tough because it's great September to December but it's really hard to keep it going through spring and summer. It's just hard to pay the bills and hard to stay on top of stuff. When I first opened Loops my goal was to be out of the branding business within a year or two but it's really, in retail it's tough. You know I tried to maintain both of these businesses. It's just the day to day grind of trying to run two businesses and wishing you could clone yourself. You know I needed three of me. I needed one of me for my kids and my husband, one of me for the yarn business and one of me for the branding business. And I just knew I couldn't keep it up forever and really give 100% to the real passion. I mean because the branding business was bringing in the money and the yarn business was where my passion was. It was a struggle for me because you know I need to put food on the table and you know it's just six months out of the year I was living in fear like how am I going to get to the next month. There was a point where it was like it was either going to be the money or the passion and I was going to have to choose.

## [03:01 - 06:04]

And then when I first watched that very first Jeff Walker video and I found Product Lunch Formula I had no idea how it was going to really transform my life. So I started watching I thought well I'll just give this a couple minutes of my time. And before I knew it it was like an hour later and I was soaking up as much of the free content that was out there as I possibly could. And then as I got a little deeper into the content it dawned on me I had seen this before without even realizing it. And I had been up late at night and seen some of Will Hamilton's videos with the Bryan Brothers and I thought wow this is really great content. I mean I'm a fairly advanced tennis player but it was really helpful content. I couldn't believe he was giving it away for free. And then along came an offer and I bought into it. And I've been in branding for 30 years you know I consider myself pretty knowledgeable and pretty jaded and when it dawned on me that that process had actually worked on me and delivered and given me real value I thought okay I'm on to something here. I've got to buy into this program. From the very first video it's like I could not get enough. It was like I was binge watching it, watching it one video after another taking notes like a madman. And then you know really I was on the very first module when the idea of Loops Club came to me. So Loops Club is an auto ship program that solves the problem for us of the seasonality of the yarn business. And it also puts a whole bunch of predictability into the business that isn't there for most retail businesses. Every month people get a really cool knitting project in the mail. They get the yarn, they get a pattern and they get sometimes little surprises like on trend effortless knitting projects. So they're not really hard but they're really nice yarns. Actually in that moment when I was deciding whether to invest or not it was like well I could spend a bunch of extra money buying Christmas gifts from my family because it was right before the holidays or I could invest in this and sort of give them their mom back like a mom who's engaged and excited and happy and you know not working around with the fear of how am I going to pay the bills next day. Every day I get up and I'm excited to create the next project and negotiate the next yarn deal and read the comments from our Loops Club members about how much they're enjoying the process.
